cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.subtitle: Understand how we use cookies to improve your experience on the platform.

s1.title: 1. What are Cookies?
s1.content: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. They allow the site to recognize your browser, remember your preferences, and improve your user experience.

s2.title: 2. Types of Cookies We Use
s2.content:
Essential Cookies: Required for the basic operation of the platform, such as keeping your session authenticated. They cannot be disabled.

Preference Cookies: Remember your settings, such as language, theme, and layout preferences.

Analytics Cookies: Help us understand how users interact with the platform. Data is anonymized.

Performance Cookies: Used to monitor platform performance and identify possible issues.

s3.title: 3. Third-Party Cookies
s3.content: Some services integrated with MakeStudio may set their own cookies, including Google Analytics, OAuth2 providers (Google, Facebook, Apple) during authentication, and customer support services.

s4.title: 4. How to Manage Cookies
s4.content:
Browser settings: Most browsers let you view, delete, and block cookies.

Preference Center: In your account settings, you can manage non-essential cookie preferences.

Warning: Disabling essential cookies may affect the proper functioning of the platform.

s5.title: 5. Data Retention
s5.content: Session cookies expire when you close the browser. Authentication cookies persist for up to 30 days. Preference cookies for up to 1 year. Analytics cookies for up to 2 years.

s6.title: 6. Updates to this Policy
s6.content: This Cookie Policy may be updated periodically. We will notify you of significant changes through a notice on the platform or by email.

s7.title: 7. Contact
s7.content: If you have questions about our Cookie Policy, contact us at privacidade@makestudio.com.br or visit our Help Center.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.text: Democratize access to intelligent automation for companies of all sizes, making artificial intelligence accessible and practical to solve real business problems.

story.title: Our Story
story.p1: MakeStudio was born from the frustration of seeing companies spending significant resources building and maintaining simple integrations and automations. We noticed a gap between very simple automation tools (which limit possibilities) and full custom development (which is expensive and time-consuming).
story.p2: In 2023, we brought together a team of AI, automation, and software development experts to create a platform that combined the best of both worlds: the simplicity of low-code tools with the power and flexibility of custom code.
story.p3: Today, thousands of companies across Brazil use MakeStudio to automate everything from customer service to complex internal processes, saving time and resources while delivering better experiences for their customers.

lgpd.overviewText: MakeStudio is fully committed to protecting the personal data of our users and complying with the Brazilian General Data Protection Law (LGPD - Law 13.709/2018).

principles.title: Purpose
principles.description: We process data only for specific purposes informed to the data subject

principles.title: Adequacy
principles.description: Processing compatible with the stated purposes

principles.title: Necessity
principles.description: Limited to the minimum necessary to achieve the purposes

principles.title: Transparency
principles.description: Clear, accurate, and accessible information about processing

principles.title: Security
principles.description: Technical and administrative measures to protect data

principles.title: Non-discrimination
principles.description: Prohibition of processing for discriminatory purposes

lgpd.rightsTitle: Your Rights as a Data Subject

rights.title: 1. Confirmation and Access
rights.description: You may confirm whether we process your personal data and request access to it.

rights.title: 2. Correction
rights.description: Request correction of incomplete, inaccurate, or outdated data.

rights.title: 3. Anonymization, Blocking, or Deletion
rights.description: Request anonymization, blocking, or deletion of unnecessary, excessive, or non-compliant data.

rights.title: 4. Portability
rights.description: Request portability of your data to another service or product provider.

rights.title: 5. Deletion
rights.description: Request deletion of data processed based on your consent.

rights.title: 6. Information about Sharing
rights.description: Obtain information about public and private entities with whom we share your data.

rights.title: 7. Consent Withdrawal
rights.description: Revoke consent for data processing at any time.

lgpd.legalBasisIntro: We process your personal data based on the following legal grounds:

legalBasisItems.text: For sending marketing communications and opt-in features
To provide our services as contracted
To comply with tax and regulatory obligations
For product improvements, security, and fraud prevention

lgpd.retentionIntro: We keep your personal data for as long as necessary to:
retentionItems: Fulfill the purposes described in this policy
Meet legal and regulatory requirements (usually 5 years for tax data)
Resolve disputes and enforce our agreements
lgpd.retentionEnd: After the retention period, your data will be anonymized or securely and irreversibly deleted.
lgpd.incidentText: In the event of a security incident that may cause relevant risk or harm to data subjects, we will notify the National Data Protection Authority (ANPD) and affected data subjects within an appropriate timeframe, as required by the LGPD.
lgpd.dpoIntro: We have appointed a Data Protection Officer (DPO) as required by the LGPD. To exercise your rights or clarify questions about data protection:
lgpd.dpoResponseTime: We will respond to your requests within 15 calendar days, which may be extended by an additional 15 days with express justification.

credentialTypes.oauth2: Modern authentication protocol used by Google, Facebook, GitHub, and other services. Allows secure access without sharing passwords through scoped access tokens.
credentialTypes.oauth1: Older OAuth version still used by some legacy APIs such as Twitter. Uses cryptographic signatures to validate requests without exposing credentials.
credentialTypes.apiKey: Simple authentication method using a single key. Ideal for public APIs, third-party services, and basic integrations that do not require OAuth.
credentialTypes.bearerToken: Access token sent in the HTTP Authorization header. Common in modern REST APIs and systems that use JWT or opaque tokens for stateless auth.
credentialTypes.basicAuth: Basic HTTP authentication using a username and password encoded in Base64. Simple, but should only be used with HTTPS.
credentialTypes.jwt: JSON Web Tokens are self-contained tokens that securely carry user information. Common in modern APIs and microservices.
credentialTypes.awsCredentials: Access Key ID and Secret Access Key used to authenticate with Amazon Web Services such as S3, Lambda, DynamoDB, SQS, and others.
credentialTypes.serviceAccountJson: JSON file with Google Cloud service account credentials. Enables programmatic access to APIs like Sheets, Drive, Calendar, and Cloud Platform.
credentialTypes.sshKey: Public/private key pair for secure SSH access to remote servers. Used for deploys, private Git access, and automation.
credentialTypes.smtp: Email server configuration for sending messages, including host, port, credentials, and TLS/SSL options.
credentialTypes.database: Connection credentials for relational or NoSQL databases such as PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, and others.
credentialTypes.ftp: Access to file servers over FTP or SFTP for uploads, downloads, synchronization, and backups.
credentialTypes.certificate: SSL/TLS digital certificates in PEM or PFX format, used for HTTPS, mTLS, and digital signing.
credentialTypes.customHeader: Custom HTTP headers for proprietary authentication flows such as X-API-Key, X-Auth-Token, and other API-specific patterns.
credentialTypes.queryParams: URL parameters used for authentication in GET requests. Some legacy APIs require api_key or token in the query string.
credentialTypes.webhookSecret: Shared secret used to validate webhook signatures and ensure incoming requests are legitimate.
credentialTypes.custom: Free-form credential format for anything that does not fit the standard types. Stores any key-value combination you need.
